factors.summary: The company is gaining market share, particularly through its Onsite locations and digital initiatives.

evidence.text: We are a customer- and growth-centric organization focused on identifying unique technologies, capabilities, and supply chain solutions that get us closer to our customers and reduce the total cost of their global supply chain. factors.summary: The revenue mix has shifted towards lower-margin segments, particularly Onsite locations and non-fastener products, which have grown faster than higher-margin products. This shift has negatively impacted gross profit margins, as these segments tend to have lower gross profit percentages than the business as a whole. The impact on margins has worsened on a sequential basis.

evidence.text: We continued to experience relatively strong growth from Onsite customers and non-fastener products, each of which tend to have a lower gross profit percentage than our business as a whole. This impact widened on a sequential basis.

We continued to experience relatively strong growth from Onsite customers and non-fastener products, each of which tend to have a lower gross profit percentage than our business as a whole. This impact widened on a sequential basis.

factors.summary: Demand trends are positive, with net sales increasing by 7.5% in the first six months of 2023, driven by higher unit sales and growth at Onsite locations. The increase was attributed to business days remaining consistent, and the higher unit sales contributed to the growth.

evidence.text: Net sales increased $259.6, or 7.5%, in the first six months of 2023 when compared to the first six months of 2022. The number of business days were the same in both periods. We experienced higher unit sales during the period that contributed to the increase in net sales in the period.

We experienced higher unit sales in the second quarter of 2023 that contributed to the increase in net sales in the period. This was primarily due to growth at our Onsite locations, particularly those opened in the last two years, which more than offset lower revenues in construction and reseller end markets related to the execution of our go-to-market branch strategy.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by strong performance across both Onsite locations and eCommerce. The evidence highlights a significant increase in net sales and unit sales, as well as notable growth in eCommerce daily sales, which rose 44.7% in the second quarter of 2023.

evidence.text: Net sales increased $259.6, or 7.5%, in the first six months of 2023 when compared to the first six months of 2022. The number of business days were the same in both periods. We experienced higher unit sales during the period that contributed to the increase in net sales in the period.

Daily sales through eCommerce grew 44.7% in the second quarter of 2023 and represented 23.3% of our total sales in the period. Our goal for weighted FASTBin and FASTVend device signings in 2023 remains between 23,000 to 25,000 MEUs.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power with successful price increases, particularly in mitigating higher transportation and material costs and addressing inflationary pressures. The pricing impacts were significant, ranging from 190 to 270 basis points across the quarters, reflecting the company's ability to pass on costs to customers while maintaining generally acceptable levels of customer acceptance.

evidence.text: The overall impact of product pricing on net sales was 240 to 270 basis points during the first six months of 2023. This reflects the carryover of broad actions taken in the first quarter of 2022 and targeted actions taken in the first quarter of 2023 to mitigate the effects of higher transportation and material costs for our products as well as the impact of general inflationary conditions in the marketplace over the past twelve months.

The impact of product pricing on net sales in the second quarter of 2023 was 190 to 220 basis points compared to the second quarter of 2022. This largely reflects the impact of general inflationary conditions in the marketplace over the past twelve months and the carryover of targeted actions taken in the first quarter of 2023 to address gross margin pressure for non-fastener and non-safety products.

factors.summary: The manufacturing end market is growing the fastest, while non-residential construction is experiencing a contraction.

evidence.text: The DSR change when compared to the same period in the prior year and the percent of sales in the period were as follows: Manufacturing 12.3% 23.5% 74.7% 71.5%. Non-residential construction -5.7% 12.3% 9.2% 10.5%
The DSR Change when compared to the same period in the prior year and the percent of sales in the period were as follows: Manufacturing 10.4% vs. Non-residential construction -8.8%

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, contributing to revenue growth. However, this growth is partly offset by negative currency fluctuations and adverse weather.

evidence.text: We experienced higher unit sales during the period that contributed to the increase in net sales in the period. This was primarily due to growth at our Onsite locations, particularly those opened in the last two years, which more than offset lower revenues in construction and reseller end markets related to the execution of our go-to-market branch strategy. Foreign exchange negatively affected sales in the first six months of 2023 by approximately 50 basis points. We estimate that adverse weather reduced our growth by approximately 10 basis points during the six-month period.

We experienced higher unit sales in the second quarter of 2023 that contributed to the increase in net sales in the period. This was primarily due to growth at our Onsite locations, particularly those opened in the last two years, which more than offset lower revenues in construction and reseller end markets related to the execution of our go-to-market branch strategy.

factors.summary: The company faces challenges with higher input costs, primarily transportation and materials, which have negatively impacted margins. However, they have implemented pricing strategies and operational efficiencies to mitigate these effects, though the overall impact remains significant as evidenced by the 240 to 270 basis points decline in net sales.

evidence.text: The overall impact of product pricing on net sales was 240 to 270 basis points during the first six months of 2023. This reflects the carryover of broad actions taken in the first quarter of 2022 and targeted actions taken in the first quarter of 2023 to mitigate the effects of higher transportation and material costs for our products as well as the impact of general inflationary conditions in the marketplace over the past twelve months.
The impact of price/cost was immaterial to our gross profit percentage in the second quarter of 2023.

factors.summary: Labor costs are showing a mixed trend, with an increase in employee-related expenses driven by higher average FTE and wages, but also a slight decrease in bonus and commission payments due to slower sales and profit growth.

evidence.text: Employee-related expenses increased when compared to the first six months of 2022. This was primarily related to an increase in expenses for FMI technology to support growth in our business, as well as higher costs to maintain and upgrade facility equipment.

In the second quarter of 2023, our employee-related expenses increased when compared to the second quarter of 2022. We experienced an increase in employee base pay due to higher average FTE during the period and, to a lesser degree, higher average wages. Bonus and commission payments decreased reflecting the impact of slower sales and profit growth versus the prior year. We also experienced higher healthcare-related costs.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident in both operating efficiency and daily sales growth, though challenges remain in maintaining efficiency.

evidence.text: Our operating and administrative expenses, as a percentage of net sales, improved to 24.6% in the first six months of 2023 from 25.3% in the first six months of 2022. This is due to a decline, as a percentage of net sales, in payroll-related expenses.

Daily sales through our Onsite locations, excluding sales transferred from branches to new Onsites, grew at a high-teens rate in the second quarter of 2023 over the second quarter of 2022. This growth is due to contributions from Onsites activated and implemented in 2022 and 2023, as well as continued growth from our older Onsite locations.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a negative margin trajectory, as both gross and operating margins are compressing. This decline is attributed to lower gross profit percentages and higher organizational costs, as well as a lower customer and product mix. Additionally, insufficient operating leverage in recent quarters has exacerbated the decrease in operating margins.

evidence.text: In the first six months of 2023, our gross profit percentage declined to 45.6% from 46.5% in the first six months of 2022. The operating income, as a percentage of net sales, declined to 21.1% in the first six months of 2023 from 21.3% in the first six months of 2022.

Our gross profit, as a percentage of net sales, declined to 45.5% in the second quarter of 2023 from 46.5% in the second quarter of 2022. The operating income, as a percentage of net sales, decreased to 21.0% in the second quarter of 2023 from 21.6% in the second quarter of 2022. The operating leverage we achieved in the second quarter of 2023 was not sufficient to offset the decline in our gross profit percentage.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost reduction measures, particularly focusing on occupancy and administrative expenses, but these efforts have been partially successful. While there have been some improvements, such as a slight decrease in operating and administrative expenses as a percentage of net sales, these savings are not sufficient to fully offset cost increases, particularly in areas like IT and general insurance.

evidence.text: The percentage change in employee-related, occupancy-related, and all other operating and administrative expenses compared to the same periods in the preceding year, is outlined in the table below. Approximate Percentage of Total Operating and Administrative Expenses Six-month Period 2023 Employee-related expenses 70% to 75% 3.4% Occupancy-related expenses 15% to 20% 6.3% All other operating and administrative expenses 10% to 15% 8.9%

Our operating and administrative expenses, as a percentage of net sales, improved to 24.6% in the second quarter of 2023 from 25.0% in the second quarter of 2022. This reflected a decline, as a percentage of net sales, in employee-related expenses partly offset by an increase, as a percentage of net sales, in occupancy-related expenses.

factors.summary: The company is increasing its capital expenditures in 2023, reflecting confidence in future growth. Investments are being made in automation, fleet equipment, IT, distribution centers, and digital infrastructure.

evidence.text: During the full year of 2023, we continue to expect our investment in property and equipment, net of proceeds from sales, to be within a range of $210.0 to $230.0, increasing from $162.4 in 2022.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Investing Activities

evidence.text: This increase ... reflects primarily: (1) higher property-related spending on upgrades to and investments in automation of certain facilities, the beginning of construction of a distribution center in Utah, and investment in materials to facilitate our branch conversion projects; (2) investments in fleet equipment to support our network of heavy trucks; and (3) an increase in spending on information technology.
We had an increase in our distribution center FTE employee headcount of 181 to support increased product throughput at our facilities and to expand our local inventory fulfillment terminals (LIFTs).

We had an increase in our remaining FTE employee headcount of 272 that relates primarily to personnel investments in information technology, manufacturing, and operational support, such as purchasing and product development.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has shown consistent improvement, with net cash provided by operating activities increasing significantly both year-over-year and quarter-over-quarter. The improvement is attributed to a reduced use of working capital and normalized global supply chains.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased by $309.4 in the first six months of 2023 when compared to the first six months of 2022.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased $150.9 in the second quarter of 2023 when compared to the second quarter of 2022.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

factors.summary: Debt leverage is showing improvement, as evidenced by a decrease in total debt and a decline in the percentage of debt relative to total capital. However, there is no explicit mention of changes in debt leverage or refinancing activities in the management's discussion and analysis section.

evidence.text: Total debt on our balance sheet was $350.0 at the end of the second quarter of 2023, or 9.4% of total capital (the sum of stockholders' equity and total debt). This compares to $505.0, or 13.7% of total capital, at the end of the second quarter of 2022. This decrease is due to applying operating cash generation to the reduction of total borrowings on the balance sheet.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns have shown a decrease, primarily due to no common stock repurchases in 2023 and a reduction in the total amount returned compared to the prior year. Despite this, there is no indication of changes in buyback or dividend policies.

evidence.text: During the second quarter of 2023, we returned $199.9 to our shareholders in the form of dividends, compared to the second quarter of 2022 when we returned $227.8 to our shareholders in the form of dividends ($178.5) and purchases of our common stock ($49.3). During the second quarter of 2023, we did not repurchase any of our common stock.

evidence.text: During the last twelve months, we increased our total FTE employee headcount by 1,108. This reflects an increase in our in-market and non-in-market selling FTE employee headcount of 655 to support growth in the marketplace and sales initiatives targeting customer acquisition. We had an increase in our distribution center FTE employee headcount of 181 to support increased product throughput at our facilities and to expand our local inventory fulfillment terminals (LIFTs). We had an increase in our remaining FTE employee headcount of 272 that relates primarily to personnel investments in information technology, manufacturing, and operational support, such as purchasing and product development.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks such as slower business activity, higher input costs, and challenges in managing labor and occupancy expenses, which were driven by increased spending on technology, facility maintenance, and other operational costs. However, these risks are partially offset by strong demand and a favorable economic environment, though foreign exchange impacts and potential economic slowdowns pose additional challenges.

evidence.text: This was primarily related to an increase in expenses for FMI technology to support growth in our business, as well as higher costs to maintain and upgrade facility equipment. Combined, all other operating and administrative expenses increased in the first six months of 2023 when compared to the first six months of 2022. The increase in other operating and administrative expenses relates primarily to higher spending on information technology, expenses for travel and supplies, and higher general insurance costs. This was partly offset by lower fuel costs related to our local truck fleet.
Foreign exchange negatively affected sales in the second quarter of 2023 by approximately 40 basis points. The overall economic environment remains a tailwind for the company's business.

factors.summary: The company faces significant sensitivities to foreign exchange movements, economic conditions, and geopolitical factors, which could negatively impact near-term results. Evidence highlights the substantial negative impact of foreign exchange on sales, as well as the effects of adverse weather and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively affected sales in the first six months of 2023 by approximately 50 basis points.
The impact of product pricing on net sales was 240 to 270 basis points during the first six months of 2023.
The impact of product pricing on net sales in the second quarter of 2023 was 190 to 220 basis points compared to the second quarter of 2022.
The impact of product pricing on net sales in the second quarter of 2022 was 660 to 690 basis points.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy emphasizes expanding digital capabilities, automation, and infrastructure investments to support growth and market presence, as reflected in increased spending on property, equipment, and information technology.

evidence.text: During the full year of 2023, we continue to expect our investment in property and equipment, net of proceeds from sales, to be within a range of $210.0 to $230.0, increasing from $162.4 in 2022. This increase for the full year of 2023 reflects primarily: (1) higher property-related spending on upgrades to and investments in automation of certain facilities, the beginning of construction of a distribution center in Utah, and investment in materials to facilitate our branch conversion projects; (2) investments in fleet equipment to support our network of heavy trucks; and (3) an increase in spending on information technology.

evidence.text: Our motto is Growth Through Customer Service ® and our tagline is Where Industry Meets Innovation ™. We are a customer- and growth-centric organization focused on identifying unique technologies, capabilities, and supply chain solutions that get us closer to our customers and reduce the total cost of their global supply chain. We believe this close-to-the-customer, 'high-touch, high-tech' partnership approach is differentiated in the marketplace and allows us to gain market share in what remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: The supply chain health has shown significant improvement due to the normalization of global supply chains, which has reduced disruptions and the need for excess working capital. This improvement is evident in the decrease in inventory balances, reflecting fewer supply chain disruptions compared to the prior year.

evidence.text: Global supply chains have normalized versus the prior year, which has reduced the rate of working capital expansion necessary to support our customers' growth.

factors.summary: The company has multiple catalysts for growth, including the completion of a distribution center in Utah, which will support future growth and efficiency, and the expansion of Onsite locations and digital capabilities, driven by the signing of FASTBin and FASTVend devices. These initiatives are expected to enhance product visibility, traceability, and operational efficiency, thereby strengthening the company's growth prospects.

evidence.text: The beginning of construction of a distribution center in Utah is a significant investment in 2023.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Investing Activities

evidence.text: Our goal for weighted FASTBin and FASTVend device signings in 2023 remains between 23,000 to 25,000 MEUs. We believe the data that is created through our digital capabilities enhances product visibility, traceability, and control that reduces risk in operations and creates ordering and fulfillment efficiencies for both ourselves and our customers. As a result, we believe our opportunity to grow our business will be enhanced through the continued development and expansion of our digital capabilities.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, which is favorable for cash flow and operational efficiency. There has been a decrease in inventory balances compared to the prior year, attributed to the absence of supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Global supply chains have normalized versus the prior year, which has reduced the rate of working capital expansion necessary to support our customers' growth.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

evidence.text: The decrease in our inventory balance in the second quarter of 2023 is primarily attributable to the absence of supply disruptions from the prior year. Our response at the time was to deepen our inventory as a means of maintaining high service to our customers, particularly for imported inventory. Dissipation of these disruptions has allowed us to shorten our product ordering cycle.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements have negatively impacted sales, reducing growth by approximately 50 basis points in the first six months of 2023.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively affected sales in the first six months of 2023 by approximately 50 basis points.
Foreign exchange negatively affected sales in the second quarter of 2023 by approximately 40 basis points.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rate changes on net interest expense has been mixed. While higher interest rates have led to an increase in net interest expense over the first six months of 2023 compared to the same period in 2022, there was a slight decrease in the second quarter of 2023 compared to the second quarter of 2022.

evidence.text: Our net interest expense was $5.8 in the first six months of 2023, compared to $4.9 in the first six months of 2022. This increase was due to slightly lower average borrowings during the period being more than offset by higher average interest rates paid on those borrowings.

Our net interest expense was $2.3 in the second quarter of 2023, compared to $2.7 in the second quarter of 2022. Lower average borrowings over the period were only partially offset by higher average interest rates paid on those borrowings.

factors.summary: The economic conditions present mixed influences, with inflationary pressures and potential economic slowdowns acting as headwinds, while strong manufacturing activity and a favorable business environment provide tailwinds.

evidence.text: The impact of product pricing on net sales was 240 to 270 basis points during the first six months of 2023. This reflects the carryover of broad actions taken in the first quarter of 2022 and targeted actions taken in the first quarter of 2023 to mitigate the effects of higher transportation and material costs for our products as well as the impact of general inflationary conditions in the marketplace over the past twelve months.

The manufacturing market includes sales of products for both original equipment manufacturing (OEM), where our products are consumed in the final products of our customers, and manufacturing, repair and operations (MRO), where our products are consumed to support the facilities and ongoing operations of our customers. The non-residential construction market includes general, electrical, plumbing, sheet metal, and road contractors. Other users of our products include farmers, truckers, railroads, oil exploration companies, oil production and refinement companies, mining companies, federal, state, and local governmental entities, schools, and certain retail trades. Geographically, our branches, Onsite locations, and customers are primarily located in North America, though we continue to grow our non-North American presence as well.

factors.summary: The information on talent retention is mixed. While one subsection indicates challenges with higher turnover and skills gaps affecting operational efficiency, another suggests that talent retention may be stable with no explicit mention of shortages or turnover issues.

evidence.text: We experienced an increase in employee base pay due to higher average FTE and average wages during the period. Bonus and commission payments decreased reflecting the impact of slower sales and profit growth versus the prior year. We also experienced higher healthcare costs and, to a lesser degree, profit sharing costs.
The text does not mention talent retention challenges or successes.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2 — MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: The company is expanding its workforce, particularly in sales, distribution, and operational support roles, as part of a growth strategy. This indicates a positive direction in terms of scaling operations and increasing capacity to meet market demands.

evidence.text: The table below summarizes our FTE headcount at the end of the periods presented and the percentage change compared to the end of the prior period: Change Since: Q2 2023 Q4 2022 Q4 2022 In-market locations (branches & Onsites) 12,380 12,017 3.0% Non-in-market selling 2,613 2,459 6.3% Selling subtotal 14,993 14,476 3.6% Distribution/Transportation 3,053 2,971 2.8% Manufacturing 723 696 3.9% Organizational support personnel (1) 1,862 1,711 8.8% Non-selling subtotal 5,638 5,378 4.8% Total 20,631 19,854 3.9%

During the last twelve months, we increased our total FTE employee headcount by 1,108. This reflects an increase in our in-market and non-in-market selling FTE employee headcount of 655 to support growth in the marketplace and sales initiatives targeting customer acquisition. We had an increase in our distribution center FTE employee headcount of 181 to support increased product throughput at our facilities and to expand our local inventory fulfillment terminals (LIFTs). We had an increase in our remaining FTE employee headcount of 272 that relates primarily to personnel investments in information technology, manufacturing, and operational support, such as purchasing and product development.
